task_completion.description: Log into the wger fitness manager as admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The wger web interface is visible in a browser, showing the user is logged in (e.g., dashboard is visible or 'admin' is seen in the user menu).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the workout routines and locate 'Push-Pull-Legs'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the wger workout routines list with the 'Push-Pull-Legs' routine visible on the screen.
The browser displays the details page specifically for the 'Push-Pull-Legs' workout routine.

task_completion.description: Export the routine's logbook as a PDF.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A PDF viewer, print dialog, or export screen is visible showing the logbook format (e.g., tracking tables/grids) for the 'Push-Pull-Legs' routine.
A file save dialog, browser download manager, or file explorer window confirms the file is named 'Push-Pull-Legs-Logbook.pdf' and is saved in the Downloads directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with wger.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the wger interface, rather than terminal curl commands or python scripts making API calls.

integrity.description: The PDF was generated from the wger application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported file or print preview visually resembles a wger logbook (tables/grids for tracking workouts), not a blank file or manually typed text document.

integrity.description: The agent did not create a fake file using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no terminal window showing commands like 'touch /home/ga/Downloads/Push-Pull-Legs-Logbook.pdf' or 'echo ... > ...pdf' to bypass the actual export process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected workout routine name is 'Push-Pull-Legs'. The exported logbook PDF must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Downloads/Push-Pull-Legs-Logbook.pdf'.
